Amendments2017—Subsec. (f)(4)(C)(xiv) to (xvii). redesignated cls. (xv) to (xvii) as (xiv) to (xvi), respectively, and struck out former cl. (xiv). Prior to amendment, text of cl. (xiv) read as follows: “For purposes of this subpargraph, the end of the initial temporary period shall be determined without regard to section 149(d)(3)(A)(iv).”Pub. L. 115–972006—Subsec. (f)(4)(D)(ii)(II) to (IV). redesignated subcls. (III) and (IV) as (II) and (III), respectively, and struck out former subcl. (II) which read as follows: “all bonds issued by a governmental unit to make loans to other governmental units with general taxing powers not subordinate to such unit shall, for purposes of applying such subclause to such unit, be treated as not issued by such unit.”Pub. (c)(2)(B) to (E). , redesignated subpars. (C) to (E) as (B) to (D), respectively, and struck out heading and text of former subpar. (B). L. 100–647, § 1013(a)(15)Subsec. (f)(4)(A). , (b), struck out “unless the issuer otherwise elects,” before “any amount earned” in cl. (ii) and inserted at end of subpar. (A) “In the case of an issue no bond of which is a private activity bond, clause (ii) shall be applied without regard to the dollar limitation therein if the average maturity of the issue (determined in accordance with section 147(b)(2)(A)) is at least 5 years and the rates of interest on bonds which are part of the issue do not vary during the term of the issue.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 6181(a)Subsec. (f)(4)(B)(iii)(I). , substituted “proceeds” for “aggregate face amount”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1013(a)(16)(A)Subsec. L. 100–647, § 6177(a)Subsec. (f)(4)(C). , in heading substituted “governmental units issuing $5,000,000 or less of bonds” for “small governmental units”, designated existing provision as cl. (i), inserted heading “In general”, redesignated existing cls. (i) to (iv) as subcls. (I) to (IV) and realigned their margins, struck out last sentence providing that cl. (iv) not take into account any bond which is not outstanding at the time of a later issue or which is redeemed, other than in an advance refunding, from the net proceeds of the later issue, and added cls. (ii) to (vi).Pub. L. 100–647, § 1013(a)(17)(A)Subsec. (f)(4)(C)(i)(IV). , struck out “(and all subordinate entities thereof)” after “such unit”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1013(a)(17)(B)Subsec. (f)(4)(D)(i). , inserted “for a program” before “described in section 144(b)(1)(A)” in introductory text, substituted “such program” for “such a program” in subcl. (I), and inserted at end “Amounts designated as interest on student loans shall not be taken into account in determining whether the issuer is reimbursed for such costs. Except as otherwise hereafter provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, costs described in subclause (I) paid from amounts earned as described in the first sentence of this clause may also be taken into account in determining the yield on the student loans under a program described in section 144(b)(1)(A).”Pub. L. 100–647, title VI, § 6177(c)102 Stat. 3727Nov. 10, 1988“The amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to bonds issued after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Nov. 10, 1988, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title VI, § 6181(c)102 Stat. 3729Nov. 10, 1988“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to bonds issued after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 10, 1988“(2).—Election for outstanding bondsAny issue of bonds other than private activity bonds outstanding as of the date of the enactment of this Act shall be allowed a 1-time election to apply the amendments made by subsection (b) [amending this section] to amounts deposited after such date in bona fide debt service funds of such bonds.“(3).—Definition of private activity bondFor purposes of this section and the last sentence of section 148(f)(4)(A) of the 1986 Code (as added by subsection (b)), the term ‘private activity bond’ shall include any qualified 501(c)(3) bond (as defined under section 145 of the 1986 Code).”, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title VI, § 6183(b)102 Stat. 3730Nov. 10, 1988“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to bonds issued after .”December 31, 1988

Extension of Period To Elect To Terminate Percent Penalty for Bonds Issued BeforeNovember 5, 1990, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–508, title XI, § 11701(j)(7)104 Stat. 1388–513Nov. 5, 1990“In the case of a bond issued before the date of the enactment of this Act [], the period for making the election under section 148(f)(4)(C)(viii) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by this subsection) shall not expire before the date which is 180 days after such date of enactment.”Nov. 5, 1990

Amendment to Arbitrage Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XIII, § 1301(c)100 Stat. 2654Oct. 22, 1986“The provision in the Federal income tax regulations relating to the arbitrage requirements which permits a higher yield on acquired obligations if the issuer elects to waive the benefits of the temporary period provisions shall not apply to bonds issued after .”August 31, 1986

children.text: to acquire higher yielding investments, or
to replace funds which were used directly or indirectly to acquire higher yielding investments.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “higher yielding investments” means any investment property which produces a yield over the term of the issue which is materially higher than the yield on the issue.

children.label: A
children.text: any security (within the meaning of section 165(g)(2)(A) or (B)),

children.label: B
children.text: any obligation,

children.label: C
children.text: any annuity contract,

children.label: D
children.text: any investment-type property, or

children.label: E
children.text: in the case of a bond other than a private activity bond, any residential rental property for family units which is not located within the jurisdiction of the issuer and which is not acquired to implement a court ordered or approved housing desegregation plan.

children.heading: Alternative minimum tax bonds treated as investment property in certain cases
children.label: 3 Alternative minimum tax bonds treated as investment property in certain cases

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term “investment property” does not include any tax-exempt bond.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: With respect to an issue other than an issue a part of which is a specified private activity bond (as defined in section 57(a)(5)(C)), the term “investment property” includes a specified private activity bond (as so defined).

children.heading: Safe harbor for prepaid natural gas
children.label: 4 Safe harbor for prepaid natural gas

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “investment-type property” does not include a prepayment under a qualified natural gas supply contract.

children.heading: Qualified natural gas supply contract
children.label: B Qualified natural gas supply contract
i
children.text: the annual average amount during the testing period of natural gas purchased (other than for resale) by customers of such utility who are located within the service area of such utility, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of natural gas to be used to transport the prepaid natural gas to the utility during such year.

children.heading: Natural gas used to generate electricity
children.label: C Natural gas used to generate electricity
i
children.text: only if the electricity is generated by a utility owned by a governmental unit, and

children.label: ii
children.text: only to the extent that the electricity is sold (other than for resale) to customers of such utility who are located within the service area of such utility.

children.heading: Adjustments for changes in customer base
children.label: D Adjustments for changes in customer base
I
children.text: after the close of the testing period and before the date of issuance of the issue, the utility owned by a governmental unit enters into a contract to supply natural gas (other than for resale) for a business use at a property within the service area of such utility, and

children.label: II
children.text: the utility did not supply natural gas to such property during the testing period or the ratable amount of natural gas to be supplied under the contract is significantly greater than the ratable amount of gas supplied to such property during the testing period,

children.heading: Lost customers
children.label: ii Lost customers
children.text: The average under subparagraph (B)(i) shall not exceed the annual amount of natural gas reasonably expected to be purchased (other than for resale) by persons who are located within the service area of such utility and who, as of the date of issuance of the issue, are customers of such utility.

children.heading: Ruling requests
children.label: E Ruling requests
children.text: The Secretary may increase the average under subparagraph (B)(i) for any period if the utility owned by the governmental unit establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that, based on objective evidence of growth in natural gas consumption or population, such average would otherwise be insufficient for such period.

children.heading: Adjustment for natural gas otherwise on hand
children.label: F Adjustment for natural gas otherwise on hand
I
children.text: the applicable share of natural gas held by the utility on the date of issuance of the issue, and

children.label: II
children.text: the natural gas (not taken into account under subclause (I)) which the utility has a right to acquire during such period (determined as of the date of issuance of the issue).

children.heading: Applicable share
children.label: ii Applicable share
children.text: For purposes of the clause (i), the term “applicable share” means, with respect to any period, the natural gas allocable to such period if the gas were allocated ratably over the period to which the prepayment relates.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of natural gas needed (other than for resale) by customers of such utility who are located within the service area of such utility, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of natural gas used to transport such natural gas to the utility.

children.heading: Testing period
children.label: H Testing period
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “testing period” means, with respect to an issue, the most recent 5 calendar years ending before the date of issuance of the issue.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a natural gas utility, natural gas transmission or distribution services, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an electric utility, electricity distribution services,

children.label: ii
children.text: any area within a county contiguous to the area described in clause (i) in which retail customers of such utility are located if such area is not also served by another utility providing natural gas or electricity services, as the case may be, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any area recognized as the service area of such utility under State or Federal law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), a bond shall not be treated as an arbitrage bond solely by reason of the fact that the proceeds of the issue of which such bond is a part may be invested in higher yielding investments for a reasonable temporary period until such proceeds are needed for the purpose for which such issue was issued.

children.heading: Limitation on temporary period for pooled fi­nancings
children.label: 2 Limitation on temporary period for pooled fi­nancings

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The temporary period referred to in paragraph (1) shall not exceed 6 months with respect to the proceeds of an issue which are to be used to make or finance loans (other than nonpurpose investments) to 2 or more persons.

children.heading: Shorter temporary period for loan repayments, etc.
children.label: B Shorter temporary period for loan repayments, etc.
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “3 months” for “6 months” with respect to the proceeds from the sale or repayment of any loan which are to be used to make or finance any loan. For purposes of the preceding sentence, a nonpurpose investment shall not be treated as a loan.

children.heading: Bonds used to provide construction financing
children.label: C Bonds used to provide construction financing
i
children.text: rules similar to the rules of subsection (f)(4)(C)(v) shall apply, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (A) shall be applied with respect to such portion by substituting “2 years” for “6 months”.

children.heading: Exception for mortgage revenue bonds
children.label: D Exception for mortgage revenue bonds
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any qualified mortgage bond or qualified veterans’ mortgage bond.

children.heading: Special rules for reasonably required reserve or replacement fund
children.label: d Special rules for reasonably required reserve or replacement fund

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), a bond shall not be treated as an arbitrage bond solely by reason of the fact that an amount of the proceeds of the issue of which such bond is a part may be invested in higher yielding investments which are part of a reasonably required reserve or replacement fund. The amount referred to in the preceding sentence shall not exceed 10 percent of the proceeds of such issue unless the issuer establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that a higher amount is necessary.

children.heading: Limitation on amount in reserve or replacement fund which may be financed by issue
children.label: 2 Limitation on amount in reserve or replacement fund which may be financed by issue
children.text: A bond issued as part of an issue shall be treated as an arbitrage bond if the amount of the proceeds from the sale of such issue which is part of any reserve or replacement fund exceeds 10 percent of the proceeds of the issue (or such higher amount which the issuer establishes is necessary to the satisfaction of the Secretary).

children.heading: Minor portion may be invested in higher yielding investments
children.label: e Minor portion may be invested in higher yielding investments

children.text: 5 percent of the proceeds of the issue, or

children.heading: Required rebate to the United States
children.label: f Required rebate to the United States

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A bond which is part of an issue shall be treated as an arbitrage bond if the requirements of paragraphs (2) and (3) are not met with respect to such issue. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any qualified veterans’ mortgage bond.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount earned on all nonpurpose investments (other than investments attributable to an excess described in this subparagraph), over

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount which would have been earned if such nonpurpose investments were invested at a rate equal to the yield on the issue, plus

children.label: B
children.text: any income attributable to the excess described in subparagraph (A),

children.heading: Due date of payments under paragraph (2)
children.label: 3 Due date of payments under paragraph (2)
children.text: Except to the extent provided by the Secretary, the amount which is required to be paid to the United States by the issuer shall be paid in installments which are made at least once every 5 years. Each installment shall be in an amount which ensures that 90 percent of the amount described in paragraph (2) with respect to the issue at the time payment of such installment is required will have been paid to the United States. The last installment shall be made no later than 60 days after the day on which the last bond of the issue is redeemed and shall be in an amount sufficient to pay the remaining balance of the amount described in paragraph (2) with respect to such issue. A series of issues which are redeemed during a 6-month period (or such longer period as the Secretary may prescribe) shall be treated (at the election of the issuer) as 1 issue for purposes of the preceding sentence if no bond which is part of any issue in such series has a maturity of more than 270 days or is a private activity bond. In the case of a tax and revenue anticipation bond, the last installment shall not be required to be made before the date 8 months after the date of issuance of the issue of which the bond is a part.

children.label: i
children.text: any gain or loss on the disposition of a nonpurpose investment shall be taken into account, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any amount earned on a bona fide debt service fund shall not be taken into account if the gross earnings on such fund for the bond year is less than $100,000.

children.label: I
children.text: the gross proceeds of such issue are expended for the governmental purposes for which the issue was issued no later than the day which is 6 months after the date of issuance of the issue, and

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements of paragraph (2) are met with respect to amounts not required to be spent as provided in subclause (I) (other than earnings on amounts in any bona fide debt service fund).

children.heading: Additional period for certain bonds
children.label: ii Additional period for certain bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In the case of an issue described in subclause (II), clause (i) shall be applied by substituting “1 year” for “6 months” each place it appears with respect to the portion of the proceeds of the issue which are not expended in accordance with clause (i) if such portion does not exceed 5 percent of the proceeds of the issue.

children.heading: Issues to which subclause (I) applies
children.label: II Issues to which subclause (I) applies
children.text: An issue is described in this subclause if no bond which is part of such issue is a private activity bond (other than a qualified 501(c)(3) bond) or a tax or revenue anticipation bond.

children.heading: Safe harbor for determining when proceeds of tax and revenue anticipation bonds are expended
children.label: iii Safe harbor for determining when proceeds of tax and revenue anticipation bonds are expended

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), in the case of an issue of tax or revenue anticipation bonds, the net proceeds of such issue (including earnings thereon) shall be treated as expended for the governmental purpose of the issue on the 1st day after the date of issuance that the cumulative cash flow deficit to be financed by such issue exceeds 90 percent of the proceeds of such issue.

children.heading: Cumulative cash flow deficit
children.label: II Cumulative cash flow deficit
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the term “cumulative cash flow deficit” means, as of the date of computation, the excess of the expenses paid during the period described in subclause (III) which would ordinarily be paid out of or financed by anticipated tax or other revenues over the aggregate amount available (other than from the proceeds of the issue) during such period for the payment of such expenses.

children.heading: Period involved
children.label: III Period involved
children.text: For purposes of subclause (II), the period described in this subclause is the period beginning on the date of issuance of the issue and ending on the earlier of the date 6 months after such date of issuance or the date of the computation of cumulative cash flow deficit.

children.heading: Payments of principal not to affect requirements
children.label: iv Payments of principal not to affect requirements
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, payments of principal on the bonds which are part of an issue shall not be treated as expended for the governmental purposes of the issue.

children.heading: Exception from rebate for certain proceeds to be used to finance construction expenditures
children.label: C Exception from rebate for certain proceeds to be used to finance construction expenditures

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a construction issue, paragraph (2) shall not apply to the available construction proceeds of such issue if the spending requirements of clause (ii) are met.

children.label: I
children.text: 10 percent of the available construction proceeds of the construction issue are spent for the governmental purposes of the issue within the 6-month period beginning on the date the bonds are issued,

children.label: II
children.text: 45 percent of such proceeds are spent for such purposes within the 1-year period beginning on such date,

children.label: III
children.text: 75 percent of such proceeds are spent for such purposes within the 18-month period beginning on such date, and

children.label: IV
children.text: 100 percent of such proceeds are spent for such purposes within the 2-year period beginning on such date.

children.label: I
children.text: such requirement would be met at the close of such 2-year period but for a reasonable retainage (not exceeding 5 percent of the available construction proceeds of the construction issue), and

children.label: II
children.text: 100 percent of the available construction proceeds of the construction issue are spent for the governmental purposes of the issue within the 3-year period beginning on the date the bonds are issued.

children.label: I
children.text: at least 75 percent of the available construction proceeds of such issue are to be used for construction expenditures with respect to property which is to be owned by a governmental unit or a 501(c)(3) organization, and

children.label: II
children.text: all of the bonds which are part of such issue are qualified 501(c)(3) bonds, bonds which are not private activity bonds, or private activity bonds issued to finance property to be owned by a governmental unit or a 501(c)(3) organization.

children.heading: Portions of issues used for construction
children.label: v Portions of issues used for construction
I
children.text: all of the construction expenditures to be financed by an issue are to be financed from a portion thereof, and

children.label: II
children.text: the issuer elects to treat such portion as a construction issue for purposes of this subparagraph,

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “available construction proceeds” means the amount equal to the issue price (within the meaning of sections 1273 and 1274) of the construction issue, increased by earnings on the issue price, earnings on amounts in any reasonably required reserve or replacement fund not funded from the issue, and earnings on all of the foregoing earnings, and reduced by the amount of the issue price in any reasonably required reserve or replacement fund and the issuance costs financed by the issue.

children.heading: Earnings on reserve included only for certain periods
children.label: II Earnings on reserve included only for certain periods
children.text: The term “available construction proceeds” shall not include amounts earned on any reasonably required reserve or replacement fund after the earlier of the close of the 2-year period described in clause (ii) or the date the construction is substantially completed.

children.heading: Payments on acquired purpose obligations excluded
children.label: III Payments on acquired purpose obligations excluded
children.text: The term “available construction proceeds” shall not include payments on any obligation acquired to carry out the governmental purposes of the issue and shall not include earnings on such payments.

children.heading: Election to rebate on earnings on reserve
children.label: IV Election to rebate on earnings on reserve
children.text: At the election of the issuer, the term “available construction proceeds” shall not include earnings on any reasonably required reserve or replacement fund.

children.heading: Election to pay penalty in lieu of rebate
children.label: vii Election to pay penalty in lieu of rebate

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: At the election of the issuer, paragraph (2) shall not apply to available construction proceeds which do not meet the spending requirements of clause (ii) if the issuer pays a penalty, with respect to each 6-month period after the date the bonds were issued, equal to 1½ percent of the amount of the available construction proceeds of the issue which, as of the close of such 6-month period, is not spent as required by clause (ii).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: II Termination
children.text: The penalty imposed by this clause shall cease to apply only as provided in clause (viii) or after the latest maturity date of any bond in the issue (including any refunding bond with respect thereto).

children.heading: Election to terminate 1½ percent penalty
children.label: viii Election to terminate 1½ percent penalty

children.heading: 3 percent penalty
children.label: I 3 percent penalty
children.text: The requirement of this subclause is met if the issuer pays a penalty equal to 3 percent of the amount of available construction proceeds of the issue which is not spent for the governmental purposes of the issue as of the close of such initial temporary period multiplied by the number of years (including fractions thereof) in the initial temporary period.

children.heading: Yield restriction at close of temporary period
children.label: II Yield restriction at close of temporary period
children.text: The requirement of this subclause is met if the amount of the available construction proceeds of the issue which is not spent for the governmental purposes of the issue as of the close of such initial temporary period is invested at a yield not exceeding the yield on the issue or which is invested in any tax-exempt bond which is not investment property.

children.heading: Redemption of bonds at earliest call date
children.label: III Redemption of bonds at earliest call date
children.text: The requirement of this subclause is met if the amount of the available construction proceeds of the issue which is not spent for the governmental purposes of the issue as of the earliest date on which bonds may be redeemed is used to redeem bonds on such date.

children.heading: Election to terminate 1½ percent penalty before end of temporary period
children.label: ix Election to terminate 1½ percent penalty before end of temporary period
I
children.text: the construction to be financed by a construction issue is substantially completed before the end of the initial temporary period,

children.label: II
children.text: the issuer identifies an amount of available construction proceeds which will not be spent for the governmental purposes of the issue,

children.label: III
children.text: the issuer has made the election under clause (viii), and

children.label: IV
children.text: the issuer makes an election under this clause before the close of the initial temporary period and not later than 90 days after the date the construction is substantially completed,

children.label: I
children.text: 50 percent of the amount which was not paid in accordance with clauses (vii) and (viii), plus

children.label: II
children.text: interest (at the underpayment rate established under section 6621) on the portion of the amount which was not paid on the date required for the period beginning on such date.

children.heading: Election for pooled financing bonds
children.label: xi Election for pooled financing bonds
I
children.text: the date the loan is made, in the case of loans made within the 1-year period after the date the bonds are issued, and

children.label: II
children.text: the date following such 1-year period, in the case of loans made after such 1-year period.

children.heading: Payments of principal not to affect requirements
children.label: xii Payments of principal not to affect requirements
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, payments of principal on the bonds which are part of the construction issue shall not be treated as an expenditure of the available construction proceeds of the issue.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in this clause, clause (vii)(II), and the last sentence of clause (x), this subparagraph shall not apply to any refunding bond and no proceeds of a refunded bond shall be treated for purposes of this subparagraph as proceeds of a refunding bond.

children.heading: Determination of construction portion of issue
children.label: II Determination of construction portion of issue
children.text: For purposes of clause (v), any portion of an issue which is used to refund any issue (or portion thereof) shall be treated as a separate issue.

children.heading: Coordination with rebate requirement on refunding bonds
children.label: III Coordination with rebate requirement on refunding bonds
children.text: The requirements of paragraph (2) shall be treated as met with respect to earnings for any period if a penalty is paid under clause (vii) or (viii) with respect to such earnings for such period.

children.heading: Elections
children.label: xiv Elections
children.text: Any election under this subparagraph (other than clauses (viii) and (ix)) shall be made on or before the date the bonds are issued; and, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: Time for payment of penalties
children.label: xv Time for payment of penalties
children.text: Any penalty under this subparagraph shall be paid to the United States not later than 90 days after the period to which the penalty relates.

children.heading: Treatment of bona fide debt service funds
children.label: xvi Treatment of bona fide debt service funds
children.text: If the spending requirements of clause (ii) are met with respect to the available construction proceeds of a construction issue, then paragraph (2) shall not apply to earnings on a bona fide debt service fund for such issue.

children.heading: Exception for governmental units issuing $5,000,000 or less of bonds
children.label: D Exception for governmental units issuing $5,000,000 or less of bonds
I
children.text: the issue is issued by a governmental unit with general taxing powers,

children.label: II
children.text: no bond which is part of such issue is a private activity bond,

children.label: III
children.text: 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of such issue are to be used for local governmental activities of the issuer (or of a governmental unit the jurisdiction of which is entirely within the jurisdiction of the issuer), and

children.label: IV
children.text: the aggregate face amount of all tax-exempt bonds (other than private activity bonds) issued by such unit during the calendar year in which such issue is issued is not reasonably expected to exceed $5,000,000.

children.label: I
children.text: an issuer and all entities which issue bonds on behalf of such issuer shall be treated as 1 issuer,

children.label: II
children.text: all bonds issued by a subordinate entity shall, for purposes of applying such subclause to each other entity to which such entity is subordinate, be treated as issued by such other entity, and

children.label: III
children.text: an entity formed (or, to the extent provided by the Secretary, availed of) to avoid the purposes of such subclause (IV) and all other entities benefiting thereby shall be treated as 1 issuer.

children.heading: Certain refunding bonds not taken into account in determining small issuer status
children.label: iii Certain refunding bonds not taken into account in determining small issuer status
children.text: There shall not be taken into account under subclause (IV) of clause (i) any bond issued to refund (other than to advance refund) any bond to the extent the amount of the refunding bond does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded bond.

children.heading: Certain issues issued by subordinate governmental units, etc., exempt from rebate requirement
children.label: iv Certain issues issued by subordinate governmental units, etc., exempt from rebate requirement
II
children.text: the amount which, when added to the aggregate face amount of other issues issued by such entity, does not exceed the portion of the $5,000,000 limitation under clause (i)(IV) which such governmental unit allocates to such entity.

children.heading: Determination of whether refunding bonds eligible for exception from rebate requirement
children.label: v Determination of whether refunding bonds eligible for exception from rebate requirement
I
children.text: the aggregate face amount of such issue does not exceed $5,000,000,

children.label: II
children.text: each refunded bond was issued as part of an issue which was treated as meeting the requirements of paragraphs (2) and (3) by reason of this subparagraph,

children.label: III
children.text: the average maturity date of the refunding bonds issued as part of such issue is not later than the average maturity date of the bonds to be refunded by such issue, and

children.label: IV
children.text: no refunding bond has a maturity date which is later than the date which is 30 years after the date the original bond was issued.

children.heading: Refundings of bonds issued under law prior to Tax Reform Act of 1986
children.label: vi Refundings of bonds issued under law prior to Tax Reform Act of 1986
I
children.text: such issue was issued by a governmental unit with general taxing powers,

children.label: II
children.text: no bond issued as part of such issue was an industrial development bond (as defined in section 103(b)(2), but without regard to subparagraph (B) of section 103(b)(3)) or a private loan bond (as defined in section 103()(2)(A), but without regard to any exception from such definition other than section 103()(2)(C)), andoo

children.label: III
children.text: the aggregate face amount of all tax-exempt bonds (other than bonds described in subclause (II)) issued by such unit during the calendar year in which such issue was issued did not exceed $5,000,000.

children.heading: Increase in exception for bonds financing public school capital expenditures
children.label: vii Increase in exception for bonds financing public school capital expenditures
children.text: Each of the $5,000,000 amounts in the preceding provisions of this subparagraph shall be increased by the lesser of $10,000,000 or so much of the aggregate face amount of the bonds as are attributable to financing the construction (within the meaning of subparagraph (C)(iv)) of public school facilities.

children.heading: Exemption from gross income of sum rebated
children.label: 5 Exemption from gross income of sum rebated
children.text: Gross income shall not include the sum described in paragraph (2). Notwithstanding any other provision of this title, no deduction shall be allowed for any amount paid to the United States under paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: is acquired with the gross proceeds of an issue, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not acquired in order to carry out the governmental purpose of the issue.

children.label: i
children.text: amounts received (including repayments of principal) as a result of investing the original proceeds of the issue, and

children.label: ii
children.text: amounts to be used to pay debt service on the issue.

children.heading: Penalty in lieu of loss of tax exemption
children.label: 7 Penalty in lieu of loss of tax exemption
A
children.text: no bond which is part of such issue is a private activity bond (other than a qualified 501(c)(3) bond),

children.label: B
children.text: the failure to meet such requirements is not due to willful neglect, and

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent of the amount which was not paid in accordance with paragraphs (2) and (3), plus

children.label: ii
children.text: interest (at the underpayment rate established under section 6621) on the portion of the amount which was not paid on the date required under paragraph (3) for the period beginning on such date.

children.heading: Student loan incentive payments
children.label: g Student loan incentive payments
children.text: Except to the extent otherwise provided in regulations, payments made by the Secretary of Education pursuant to section 438 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 are not to be taken into account, for purposes of subsection (a)(1), in determining yields on student loan notes.

children.heading: Determinations of yield
children.label: h Determinations of yield
children.text: For purposes of this section, the yield on an issue shall be determined on the basis of the issue price (within the meaning of sections 1273 and 1274).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
